content.problemStatement:
In a class of NNN students, a class test was held. The ithi^{th}ith student scored AiA_iAi​ marks. It is also known that the scores of all students were **distinct**.

A student passes the test if their score is **strictly greater** than the passing mark.
Given that exactly XXX students pass in the test, find the **maximum** value of the passing mark of the test.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the number of students in the class and the number of students that passed in the test.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the marks of the ithi^{th}ith student.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** value of the passing mark of the test.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and X — the number of students and the exact number of students who passed. The next line contains N distinct integers A1 … AN, the marks of the students.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line — the maximum possible passing mark such that exactly X students have a strictly higher score.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: All 2 students must pass, so the passing mark must be less than the smallest score 1. Maximum possible is 0.\nTest case 2: Only the highest score 7 should pass, so passing mark < 7 and >= next lower score 5. Maximum is 6.\nTest case 3: Top 3 scores are 31,70,100; the smallest among them is 31, so passing mark = 31-1 = 30.
All 5 students pass, so passing mark must be less than the minimum score 10. Maximum possible is 9.
Sorted scores: 1 2 3 6 8 9. Top 3 are 6,8,9; smallest among them is 6. Passing mark = 6-1 = 5.

solution.approach: Sort the marks in non‑decreasing order. After sorting, the smallest mark among the X students who must pass is at index N‑X (0‑based). The passing mark must be strictly less than this value, and to maximise it we set it to one less than that mark. Hence answer = sorted[N‑X] - 1. The algorithm runs in O(N log N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case due to sorting.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); X = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        arr.sort()
        out.append(str(arr[N - X] - 1))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
